repo.description: CLI-Anything: Making ALL Software Agent-Native -- CLI-Hub

QS-01_activity_pulse.detail: Multiple commits per day, pushed today.

QS-04_issue_health.detail: 74 open issues. Feature requests visible. No triage labels.

QS-05_pr_throughput.detail: Active merge activity. Community PRs merged regularly.

QS-07_contributor_diversity.detail: 44 contributors. Mix of maintainer and community.

QS-09_community_health.detail: 75% GitHub profile. Missing Code of Conduct.

QS-10_documentation.detail: Multi-language README, CONTRIBUTING, SECURITY, per-harness docs.

QS-11_ci_quality.detail: Only deploy-pages.yml. No test CI. Copilot review on PRs.

QS-12_security_posture.detail: SECURITY.md with threat model. 0 shell=True in 122 subprocess calls. But no SAST CI.

QS-15_code_scanning.detail: No SAST workflow for subprocess-heavy codebase.

ST-01_subprocess_safety.detail: 122 subprocess calls, 0 shell=True. Click.Path validation. Codec allowlists.

ST-02_test_coverage.detail: 2,707 test functions (1,854 unit, 740 e2e). All 31 harnesses have tests.

ST-03_test_ci_enforcement.detail: 2,707 tests exist but no CI workflow runs them.

ST-04_code_structure.detail: 31/31 harnesses follow consistent layout. 28/33 registry entries have SKILL.md.

ST-05_code_reuse.detail: repl_skin.py copied not imported. HARNESS.md SOP followed. No shared package.

ST-07_credential_handling.detail: Inconsistent: AdGuardHome JSON config vs env vars. No shared standard.

ST-08_path_safety.detail: Click.Path validation. os.path.abspath normalization. No shared traversal guard.

ST-09_type_safety.detail: Type hints in some files. No mypy/pyright/ruff config.

ST-10_dependency_isolation.detail: Each harness independently installable. Click is universal framework.

ST-11_error_handling.detail: Backend modules include RuntimeError with install instructions.

ST-12_registry_quality.detail: 33 entries with consistent schema. Categories, install commands, contributor attribution.

ST-13_methodology_docs.detail: HARNESS.md: 7-phase SOP with code examples. Novel contribution.

ST-15_monorepo_coordination.detail: No monorepo tooling. No root test runner. No shared CI matrix.

absence_patterns.evidence: SECURITY.md backed by real code mitigations (0 shell=True, allowlists). Gap is CI enforcement.
2,707 real test functions. Gap is CI automation, not test quality.

WR-01_stack_compatibility.detail: Python ecosystem. Our stack is Node/TypeScript. Individual harnesses installable via pip but not native to our toolchain. Claude Code plugin format (.claude-plugin/) is directly compatible.

WR-02_integration_complexity.detail: Plugin install is one command. Each harness is self-contained with no cross-dependencies. Claude Code marketplace.json integration is minimal config. Main friction: Python runtime required.

WR-03_maintenance_burden.detail: 26-day-old project with 1 release. No SemVer stability guarantee. Fast-moving codebase with breaking changes possible. Copy-paste repl_skin.py means updates require 31+ file touches.

WR-04_lock_in_risk.detail: Apache-2.0 license. No proprietary formats. Harnesses wrap open-source software via subprocess. Data stays local. Easy to fork or replace individual harnesses.

WR-05_value_to_cost.detail: Unique value: agent-native CLI wrapping methodology (HARNESS.md) is novel. 33 ready-made harnesses. Cost: Python dependency, immature CI/release process, maintenance uncertainty. For methodology extraction: high value, low cost. For ecosystem adoption: moderate value, moderate cost.

WR-06_ecosystem_maturity.detail: 26 days old. 27.5k stars but only 1 release. No SLA, no LTS, no enterprise adoption visible. Community growing fast but governance lags. HARNESS.md methodology is mature; project infrastructure is not.

adoption_assessment.recommendation: Trial for Claude Code plugin integration and methodology extraction. Do not adopt as core infrastructure dependency until release cadence and CI mature.
